title: Harness Configuration
description: Operator-authored configuration that controls how autoharness installs and maintains the agent harness in this workspace. Place at .autoharness/config.yaml before running autoharness install or tune.

preset.description: Installation preset controlling the default primitive set and capability pack defaults

primary_stack_pack.description: Primary stack classification to prefer when multiple additive stack packs are present. Overrides auto-detected primary stack selection when set.

stack_packs.description: Additive stack classifications to treat as active for composition. Overrides detected stack packs when specified.

install_layers.description: Explicit artifact-class layers to compose. Use to clarify or constrain preset-derived composition. The 'overlays' layer MUST be present when capability_packs is non-empty and SHOULD NOT be present when capability_packs is empty.

capability_packs.description: Capability packs to enable. Overrides preset defaults when specified.

tool.description: Backlog tool to use. When set, overrides auto-detection.

directory.description: Root directory for the backlog (relative to workspace root). Defaults to the detected tool's directory or '.backlog'.

suffix_map.description: Work item type suffixes. When using backlogit, these are read from backlogit's project config and merged here. Operator values override auto-detected values.

root.description: Root directory for durable knowledge artifacts (relative to workspace root)

subdirectories.description: Subdirectory names within the docs root. Override to match existing workspace conventions.

continuous_learning.description: Configuration for the optional continuous-learning capability pack

directory.description: Repo-local directory for observations, instincts, and evolved learned artifacts metadata

capture_hooks.description: Enable environment-specific hook capture when the workspace and selected adapter support it

environment_adapter.description: Optional environment-specific capture adapter name, such as 'copilot-hooks' or a custom adapter

promotion_threshold.description: Minimum corroborating observations before an instinct is considered ready to evolve into a learned artifact

strict_safety.description: Configuration for the optional strict-safety capability pack. Defines action classification and approval rules for risky work.

enabled.description: Whether strict-safety action classification is active

require_approval_for.description: ActionRisk levels that require explicit operator approval before execution

graphtor_docs.description: Configuration for the optional graphtor-docs capability pack. Enables indexed local documentation search via the graphtor-docs MCP server.

sources_path.description: Path (relative to workspace root) to the graphtor-docs source index configuration file

binary_path.description: Explicit path to the graphtor-docs binary. When null, the binary is resolved from PATH or the default '.graphtor/bin/' location.

model_routing.description: Model preferences for each routing tier. Agent definitions use tier labels; this maps tiers to specific model identifiers and per-tier configuration.

tier1.description: Fast/cheap tier for low-complexity tasks (memory, docs, prompt editing). Accepts a plain model-identifier string (legacy) or an object with 'model' plus optional routing metadata.

oneOf.description: Legacy: plain model identifier string. E.g., 'gpt-5.4-mini'

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort setting for Tier 1 agents (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high'). Leave empty to use the model default.

model_provider.description: Model provider for Tier 1 agents (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic'). Used when environment supports multi-provider routing.

model_family.description: Model family shorthand for Tier 1 (e.g., 'gpt-5.4-mini', 'haiku'). Resolved into agent frontmatter at install time.

tier2.description: Standard tier for routine tasks (orchestration, code writing, review). Accepts a plain model-identifier string (legacy) or an object with 'model' plus optional routing metadata.

oneOf.description: Legacy: plain model identifier string. E.g., 'claude-sonnet-4'

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort setting for Tier 2 agents (e.g., 'medium', 'high'). Leave empty to use the model default.

tier3.description: Frontier tier for complex tasks (planning, architecture, analysis). Accepts a plain model-identifier string (legacy) or an object with 'model' plus optional routing metadata.

oneOf.description: Legacy: plain model identifier string. E.g., 'claude-opus-4.6'

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort setting for Tier 3 agents (e.g., 'high', 'max'). Leave empty to use the model default.

orchestrator.description: Per-agent model override for the Orchestrator. When set, the orchestrator uses this model instead of inheriting from tier2. Accepts a plain model-identifier string or an object with routing metadata.

model.description: Model identifier for the Orchestrator. Optional — not consumed by agent frontmatter directly but recorded for documentation and tooling.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort setting for the Orchestrator (e.g., 'high'). Leave empty to use the model default.

model_family.description: Model family shorthand for the Orchestrator (e.g., 'gpt-5.4', 'claude-sonnet-4.6'). Default: gpt-5.4.

ai_tools.description: AI CLI tool configuration used when generating workspace startup scripts (start.ps1 / start.sh).

exe_path.description: Path to the Copilot CLI executable (no arguments). Defaults to 'copilot', which expects the binary on PATH. Use a full path when copilot is not on PATH, e.g. C:\Tools\ghcpcli\copilot.exe

primitives.description: Specific primitive numbers to install (1-10). Overrides preset when specified.

overrides.description: Explicit overrides for template variables that take precedence over auto-detected values. Use sparingly — prefer correcting the workspace setup over overriding discovery.
